title: ARCHIVED – Settlement Program and Resettlement Assistance Program: Call for proposals (CFP) 2024 - Information sessions (webinars) - Canada.ca
content:
## ARCHIVED – Settlement Program and Resettlement Assistance Program: Call for proposals (CFP) 2024 - Information sessions (webinars)

## We have archived this page and will not be updating it.
You can use it for research or reference.

## We have archived this page and will not be updating it.
You can use it for research or reference.
We’re no longer offering webinars.
- For questions about CFP 2024, email us at cfp-adp@cic.gc.ca .
- For technical questions about the Grants and Contributions System online application, contact us at gcs-ssc@cic.gc.ca .

## Before you sign up for a webinar
Make sure you first carefully read the Introduction
            and overview to learn about how CFP 2024 works.  If you plan to attend a targeted session, make sure to
        also read the specific funding
            guidelines of the funding opportunities of interest to you.
We will be hosting a variety of webinars in both official languages during the application period to provide you with
    the opportunity to ask questions relating to the national Call for Proposals (CFP) 2024 funding process.
We encourage you to join as many webinars as you wish, and recommend you select those that align with your
    prospective application(s).

### Types of sessions available
- General information sessions for new applicants
- Targeted sessions regarding funding opportunities (e.g. specific service or stream)
- Drop-in sessions - question and answer format Prior to attending any drop-in sessions, you should have already attended a webinar. Drop-in sessions are to be used as an opportunity to ask our subject matter experts any outstanding
                    questions you may have. These sessions will be structured in more of a discussion format, rather than an information
                            session. You should join this webinar if you would like to ask any questions directly to our subject
                            matter experts, or simply listen to the questions asked and the responses provided. Please
                            note that there is a Frequently asked questions page for funding applicants. We recommend
                            you visit this page as there may be answers that are helpful to your application.
- Prior to attending any drop-in sessions, you should have already attended a webinar.
- Drop-in sessions are to be used as an opportunity to ask our subject matter experts any outstanding
                    questions you may have. These sessions will be structured in more of a discussion format, rather than an information
                            session. You should join this webinar if you would like to ask any questions directly to our subject
                            matter experts, or simply listen to the questions asked and the responses provided. Please
                            note that there is a Frequently asked questions page for funding applicants. We recommend
                            you visit this page as there may be answers that are helpful to your application.
- These sessions will be structured in more of a discussion format, rather than an information
                            session.
- You should join this webinar if you would like to ask any questions directly to our subject
                            matter experts, or simply listen to the questions asked and the responses provided. Please
                            note that there is a Frequently asked questions page for funding applicants. We recommend
                            you visit this page as there may be answers that are helpful to your application.
Specific dates, times descriptions, and registration details for each webinar can be found in the table below.

### How to register for a webinar
You are required to register via the Microsoft Teams links for the webinars you wish to attend.
- Click on the webinar session name in the table below to access the registration link.
- Ask questions using the Slido link and event code for that
        session

You should join this webinar if you are:
- new to applying for IRCC funding;
- have never received IRCC funding; or
- are a new organization seeking to offer (re)settlement services to newcomers.
This webinar is an opportunity to learn more about:
- the 2024 Call for proposals,
- how to navigate our online Grants and Contributions System (GCS), and
- tips on writing your application for funding.
It will provide basic information only.
We encourage you to attend any one of the other webinars to get the most complete picture of IRCC
            requirements for the 2024 Call for proposals.
November 21, 2023 11:00 am (EST)
This webinar has taken place.

November 21, 2023 1:30 pm (EST)
This webinar has taken place.
You should join this webinar if you plan to submit an application under one or more of the following program
            areas:
- Pre-arrival Services
- Needs and Assets Assessment and Referral Services (NAARS)
- Case Management Services
- Information and Orientation
- Employment-related Services
- Community Connections
- Settlement Workers In Schools (SWIS)
- Indirect Services with a National or Sector Focus (e.g. umbrella organizations)
- Indirect Services with a Community or Local Focus (e.g. Local Immigration Partnerships (LIPs))
This webinar is an opportunity to learn more about IRCC’s priorities for the Settlement Program, and what is
            new in each of these program areas.

This webinar has taken place.

You should join this webinar if your proposal(s) aligns with this Stream.
            The Equity stream is a dedicated stream within the Settlement Program to provide funding to established
            organizations to support not-currently funded grassroots organizations.
- The intention is to increase diversity and representation of organizations involved in newcomer
                settlement and integration It aims to provide funding to organizations with demonstrated expertise in
                the Settlement sector to act as intermediaries to support the organizational capacity-building of
                grassroots organizations that serve or are led by equity-deserving populations, to subsequently increase
                their participation in the Settlement Sector.
December 6, 2023 1:30 pm (EST)
This webinar has taken place.

December 6, 2023 11:00 am (EST)
This webinar has taken place.
You should join this webinar if you plan to submit an application(s) under the Resettlement Assistance
            Program (RAP). RAP is a funding program operating in all provinces outside of Quebec.
- This program supports Government-Assisted Refugees (GARs) and other eligible clients upon arrival in
                Canada by providing them with direct financial support, and by funding service provider organizations to
                deliver immediate and essential services. This includes the provision of temporary accommodation and
                assistance finding permanent housing, orientation to life in Canada, and registration and referrals to
                other government and community services.
This webinar is an opportunity to learn more about RAP, and understand whether the application(s) you
                are working on meet eligible criteria and aligns with the immediate outcomes of RAP services.

This webinar has taken place.

You should join this webinar if your proposal(s) aligns with this Stream.
            The Service Delivery Improvement (SDI) stream is a dedicated stream within the Settlement Program to build
            evidence to support future settlement programming.
- It aims to fund larger-scale pilot projects that will test promising practices or concepts identified
                through previous SDI Expression of Interest (EOI) processes.
- The goal of these pilots is to generate outcomes that are evidence-based and validate the effectiveness
                and efficiency of the promising practices or concepts.
December 5, 2023 11:00 am (EST)
This webinar has taken place.
You should join this webinar if your proposal(s) aligns with this Stream.

December 5, 2023 1:30 pm (EST)
This webinar has taken place.
You should join this webinar if your proposal(s) align with this stream.
The Francophone Integration Pathway (FIP) is a suite of settlement services in French offered in a concerted
            manner by Francophone organizations and which includes all direct and indirect services of the Settlement
            Program as well as those of the Resettlement Program.
- The goal of the FIP is to create the best possible conditions so that French-speaking newcomers can
                settle and thrive in French and contribute to Canadian society and the vitality of French-speaking
                communities. The goal is to connect them to French-speaking communities so that they can be referred to
                services in French, so that they can be served in the official language in which they are most
                comfortable to have the best chance in facilitating their integration process.
This webinar is an opportunity to find out more about FIP.
December 7, 2023 1:30 pm (EST)
This webinar has taken place.
You should join this webinar if your proposal(s) align with this stream.

Prerequisite: You should have already attended a webinar regarding the specific service or stream.
You should join this webinar if you would like to ask any questions directly to our subject matter experts,
            or simply listen to the questions asked and the responses provided.
- Drop-in sessions are to be used as an opportunity to ask our subject matter experts any outstanding
                questions you may have.
- These sessions will be structured in more of a discussion format, rather than an information session.
Please note that there is also a Frequently
                asked questions page. We recommend you visit this page as there may be answers that are helpful to
            your application.
